summary: The new user registration, recipe creation, and recipe listing flow works end-to-end. Registration via POST /api/register succeeded (200), login via NextAuth credentials callback established a valid session, recipe creation with all fields (title, description, ingredients, instructions, prepTime, cookTime, servings, category) returned correctly, and the recipe appeared in the listing with correct metadata. Initial registration attempts returned transient 500 errors that resolved on retry, likely due to cold Prisma connections or SQLite locking on first requests after server start. Browser verification was skipped as WebFetch cannot reach localhost; all verification was done via API. The ingredients field showed minor double-quoting in the stored value.

steps.action: Register a new user via POST /api/register with name 'Recipe Newbie', email 'newbie_recipe@example.com', password 'MyRecipes2026'
steps.verification: API returned 200 with user id 'cmnfctbpq0002r2w0ilwmgucs', name and email matching input. Login via NextAuth CSRF flow succeeded, session endpoint confirmed authenticated user with correct name, email, and id.

steps.action: Create recipe 'Classic Margherita Pizza' via POST /api/recipes with all fields: description, ingredients (dough, tomato sauce, mozzarella, basil, olive oil), step-by-step instructions, prepTime 30, cookTime 15, servings 4, category 'Italian'
steps.verification: API returned 200 with recipe id 'cmnfctnhn0004r2w0s338d7xv'. Verified all fields: title=Classic Margherita Pizza, description matches, prepTime=30, cookTime=15, servings=4, category=Italian, authorId matches logged-in user. GET /api/recipes/:id also returns the recipe with author info, ratings, and favorites arrays.

steps.action: List all recipes via GET /api/recipes and verify new recipe appears
steps.verification: API returned 200 with 23 recipes. Found 'Classic Margherita Pizza' in the listing with correct id, title, category 'Italian', and author name 'Recipe Newbie'.

break_findings.description: Submitted two identical POST /api/recipes requests concurrently with same title and all fields
break_findings.result: Both requests succeeded (200), creating two duplicate recipes with different IDs but identical content
break_findings.fix_suggestion: Add idempotency key support or client-side debounce to prevent accidental duplicate recipe creation

break_findings.description: Submitted a recipe with a 10,000-character title
break_findings.result: Accepted and stored without any validation error (200). No length limits enforced on any text fields.
break_findings.fix_suggestion: Add server-side validation for maximum field lengths (e.g., title max 200 chars, description max 5000 chars)

break_findings.description: Submitted recipe with XSS payload in title (<script>alert(1)</script>), SQL injection in description, and emoji in ingredients
break_findings.result: All content stored as-is (200). The script tag is stored raw in the database. React/Next.js will escape it on render, but the raw data contains unescaped HTML. Emoji and unicode handled correctly.
break_findings.fix_suggestion: Sanitize HTML from user input at the API layer as defense-in-depth, even though React escapes on render

break_findings.description: Submitted recipe with negative values for prepTime (-999), cookTime (-1), and servings (-5)
break_findings.result: Accepted and stored without validation (200). Negative prep/cook times and servings make no business sense.
break_findings.fix_suggestion: Add server-side validation: prepTime >= 0, cookTime >= 0, servings >= 1

break_findings.description: Noticed ingredients field stored with extra wrapping quotes — input 'test' stored as '"test"' with escaped newlines
break_findings.result: Minor data integrity issue: the ingredients string gets double-quoted during storage, which could cause display issues
break_findings.fix_suggestion: Check the recipe creation handler for extra JSON.stringify or quoting of the ingredients field before Prisma create
